subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear table of contents with numbered headings gives a solid skeleton
Recognisable introduction and conclusion with logical flow through sections
Descriptive headings like âMathematical Model for Edge Detectionâ help orient the reader
weaknesses: Mis-numbered headings in the table of contents and appendices blending into main text
Some digressions, typographical errors and repeated explanations reduce conciseness
Cover page missing essential student details and figure captions contain grammatical errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place two strands firmly in band 3 (coherence and organisation) and one in band 2 (conciseness). The structure is good and transitions are generally clear, but mis-numbering, elementary digressions and repetition prevent an âexceptionalâ band-4 rating. A score of 3 out of 4 accurately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Consistent use of key symbols (âI, âI/âx, Î¸) for the majority of the work
Effective integration of multiple representations (tables, annotated images, plots) to support understanding
Generally clear logical progression from definitions to formulae to coded calculations
weaknesses: Notation inconsistencies (mixing I and l, occasional mis-labelled axes) disrupt full clarity
Axis labels and scales are sometimes omitted in figures
Small logical leaps when introducing Sobel filters without full proof
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands achieve band-3 performance (representations, clarity) while notation is at band-2. The work consistently communicates mathematics at a good level, but occasional inconsistencies and missing labels prevent a band-4. A score of 3 out of 4 reflects effective but not flawless communication.

strengths: Clear personal motivation articulated through interest in photography
Creative initiative in selecting a personal cat image and scripting Python routines
Noticeable independent thinking in customizing the 10Ã10 crop and visualization
weaknesses: Methodology (first-order gradients) follows standard tutorials rather than novel approaches
No comparative study or exploration of alternative algorithms
Personal approach is clear but not exceptionally innovative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands show significant evidence of engagement and initiative but do not reach âoutstandingâ or âexceptionalâ levels. The exploration merits band-2 across independent thinking, personal approach and creativity, yielding 2 out of 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on noise sensitivity and threshold choice linked to the aim
Thoughtful analysis of gradient magnitude and direction in relation to edges
Clear evaluation of limitations with sensible improvements (Gaussian blur, Canny)
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive rather than deeply critical or quantitative
Conclusion bullet points lack a synthesized narrative
No exploration of error sources or alternative models in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently place the work in band-2 for depth of reflection, critical analysis and evaluation of outcomes. Comments are meaningful but not substantial or deeply insightful, supporting a 2 out of 3 score.

strengths: Relevant partial derivatives and gradient calculations drive the data processing
Mathematical level fully commensurate with HL Analysis & Approaches syllabus
Numerous correct worked examples (|âI|â14.14, Î¸=â45Â°) demonstrate thorough understanding
Strong foundation in discrete-difference approach with solid explanations
weaknesses: Several typographical mistakes (duplicated values, mis-labelled symbols) indicate minor accuracy issues
Sobel operator convolution kernels are mentioned without derivation
Minor inconsistencies in formula notation across sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance and level of mathematics are firmly in band-4, with strong and mostly correct application. The quantity and clarity of worked examples, combined with effective use throughout, push the work toward band-5 (âcomprehensively appliedâ), despite a few typographical errors. A score of 5 out of 6 best reflects the high standard and real-world application of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally coherent: definitions lead to formulae, then to coded calculations and finally to interpretations. Explanations are clear enough for a reader with HL background, but small logical slips (e.g. mis-labelled examples, brief jump from derivatives to Sobel without proof) limit clarity to âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ.

Calculations are generally correct and examples are worked accurately, but several typographical mistakes (duplicated âI/âx values, mis-labelled symbols) indicate minor accuracy issues. Conceptual understanding of gradients is sound, so the work fits âgood understanding with minor errorsâ.

The exploration integrates numerical tables, annotated images, gradient-direction arrow plots and formulae. These multiple representations genuinely aid understanding of the mathematics, although axis labels and scales are sometimes omitted; overall the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The report follows the canonical IA flow (introduction-background-model-data-calculation-results-conclusion) and ideas progress in a sensible order. Transitions between sections are generally clear and the reader can follow how each part builds on the previous one. Minor digressions, typographic errors and some duplication (e.g. repeated explanations of Î¸) interrupt the flow, preventing the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Implementing code to crop, compute gradients and visualise vectors reflects creativity and initiative beyond manual calculation. Still, the exploration does not push into novel algorithms or comparative studies, so the initiative is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

The student analyses gradient tables and discusses how high |âI| pinpoints edges, offering thoughtful but not profound insight. There is some comparison of different Î¸ values, yet no exploration of error sources or alternative models, so the analysis merits band 2.

Limitations (resolution, threshold, noise) and suggestions (Gaussian blur, Canny) show the student evaluates outcomes and proposes sensible improvements. However, the evaluation stays qualitative; no quantitative test of suggested changes is presented. Thus the strand aligns with the âclear evaluationâ descriptor.

Reflection sections summarise findings, acknowledge noise-sensitivity and threshold choice, and link results back to the aim. These comments are meaningful, but they remain descriptive rather than deeply critical, placing the work in band 2.

Key symbols (âI, âI/âx, Î¸) are used correctly, but the student sometimes writes l instead of I, repeats x when y is meant, and mixes bold and italic. Definitions of terms are occasionally informal. Hence appropriate notation is present but not consistent enough for band 3.

The student frames the investigation around personal interest in photography and tailors data collection (image choice, region of interest) accordingly. This shows a clear personal approach, yet methodology and structure remain conventional, so the work sits solidly in band 2.

Most content is relevant to edge detection, but lengthy pedagogical explanations of basic pixel concepts, repeated bullet lists and an over-long bibliography reduce conciseness. Occasional digressions (e.g. career aspirations) and duplication mean the work is âmostly relevantâ rather than âconcise throughoutâ.

First-order partial derivatives, gradient magnitude/direction, thresholding and matrix handling are all directly relevant to edge detection and applied throughout. The mathematics is not merely decorative; it drives data processing and interpretation, satisfying the ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ descriptor.

Choosing a personal photograph, coding Python routines and selecting a 10Ã10 crop demonstrate noticeable independent thinking beyond textbook examples. However, the mathematical path (first-order gradients for edge detection) closely follows standard tutorials, so the evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

A detailed table of contents, numbered headings and consistent sub-headings give the work a clear skeleton. Figures, tables and the appendix are referenced (though not always precisely) and the introduction and conclusion are recognisable. However, some headings are mis-numbered and appendices blend into the main text, so the organisation, while good, is not âexceptionalâ.

Partial derivatives, discrete approximations and vector calculus are squarely within HL Analysis & Approaches scope, and are applied to a real dataset. While not especially advanced, the level is fully commensurate with the course, hence band 4 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The Conclusion section lists bullet points without a synthesized narrative. Expanding these into coherent prose will deepen reflection.
Bibliography entries are presented as headings only. Adopting a consistent citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) and full references will improve presentation.
The aim of the exploration is stated but not clearly labeled as the research question. Explicitly highlight the central question for reader clarity.
Figure 1.0âs caption contains grammatical errors (âvalvesâ instead of âvaluesâ). Revising the caption will enhance professionalism.
The title clearly articulates the topic and highlights the use of gradients and calculus in image analysis, setting a strong focus for the exploration.
The cover page omits essential student details such as candidate number and date. Adding these will align with IB presentation requirements.
Several headings in the Table of Contents appear mis-numbered (e.g., Section 2 appears under â1. Introductionâ). Ensure heading numbers match the actual structure.
The Table of Contents is well placed but would benefit from exact page references and consistent formatting for rapid navigation.
The studentâs personal motivation for photography and exploration into edge detection is clearly articulated, demonstrating genuine engagement.
The introduction ends abruptly mid-sentence (âand digitalâ). Ensure the final thought is completed and proofread to avoid truncation.
The detailed explanation of pixel brightness (0â255) is elementary and repeated later. Condensing this background will improve conciseness.
The 3Ã3 pixel grid appears without a caption or table number. Adding âTable 1: Sample Pixel Valuesâ will improve referencing and clarity.
The figure showing pixel-to-image mapping is effective, but the image lacks axis labels or scale, making it hard to evaluate the mapping precisely.
The magnitude is computed correctly, but more commentary on how this value relates to threshold selection would deepen your analysis.
The formula Î¸=arctan((âI/ây)/(âI/âx)) is presented correctly, but clarify branch choice and units (degrees vs radians).
Your analogy comparing edge detection to coloring-book outlines is engaging and effectively builds reader intuition.
Introduce the Sobel operatorâs convolution kernels when first mentioning edge filters to bridge theory with practical implementation.
Notation inconsistency: earlier you use I(x,y) but here the text shows âl/ây. Use I consistently to avoid confusion.
The presentation of the gradient vector âI=(âI/âx,âI/ây) is correct and provides a solid foundation for subsequent calculations.
A brief derivation showing how Sobel filter matrices approximate these derivatives would strengthen the link between calculus and convolution.
The heading âMathematical Model for Edge Detectionâ is aptly descriptive and helps orient the reader to the core methodology.
The 3Ã3 pixel grid table is not referenced or discussed directly in the text, and its placement is misaligned. Link text and table for clarity.
The general definition âf(a)/âx uses f(a), but here you should specify f(a)=I(x,y) to maintain mathematical clarity in context.
The explanatory list on discrete pixel sampling effectively highlights the rationale for În=1, helping the reader follow the finite-difference approach.
The derivative expression În=1 then âI/ânâI(n+1,h)âI(n,h) is correct, but earlier you define horizontal change as I(x+1)âI(xâ1). Ensure consistency in formulas.
The worked example calculating |âI|=â(10Â²+(â10)Â²)â14.14 is correctly executed and clearly shown.
Correctly computing Î¸=arctan(â10/10)=â45Â° demonstrates solid grasp of gradient direction in context.
The use of a negative angle convention is fine, but the reader would benefit from an explicit note on how clockwise vs counterclockwise angles are handled.
Selecting a personal cat image and focusing on a 10Ã10 crop shows initiative and creativity in data collection.
